prompt:
Write a PR description for my branch. Here's what changed:

I'm adding OAuth2 login with Google to our Express.js app. Currently we only have email/password auth. The branch has 8 changed files:

- New: src/auth/oauth2.ts (OAuth2 client setup, token exchange, callback handler)
- New: src/auth/providers/google.ts (Google-specific profile mapping)
- Modified: src/auth/router.ts (added /auth/google and /auth/google/callback routes)
- Modified: src/middleware/session.ts (extended session to store OAuth tokens)
- Modified: src/models/user.ts (added google_id, avatar_url, auth_provider fields)
- New: migrations/20260305_add_oauth_fields.sql (ALTER TABLE users ADD columns)
- New: tests/auth/oauth2.test.ts (15 new tests)
- Modified: config/default.yaml (added oauth2 section with client_id placeholder)

The motivation is that 60% of our beta users requested social login in the feedback survey. We chose Google first because 80% of our users already have Google accounts based on email domain analysis.

This is going to production next week.
assertions: Description includes a Summary section with a 1-3 sentence overview mentioning OAuth2 or Google login
Description includes a Motivation section explaining WHY (user feedback, 60% requested social login)
Description includes a Changes section that groups changes by concept (auth flow, data model, configuration) rather than listing files one by one
Description includes a Testing Instructions section with numbered, copy-pasteable steps for verifying the OAuth flow
Description includes a Rollback Plan section that addresses the database migration (ALTER TABLE) and how to reverse it
Description includes a Reviewer Notes section that flags security-sensitive areas (token handling, OAuth callback, session management)
Testing instructions include at least one edge case or error scenario (e.g., user denies consent, invalid token, duplicate Google account)
Rollback plan mentions the database migration specifically and whether it is reversible
